content:
Letting each \(A_i\in\{2,3\}\), one checks that in order to have \(\sum A_i=19\) we need exactly five 3’s and two 2’s.  To minimize 
\[
M=\max_i|A_i-a_i|,
\]
we choose the two smallest \(a_i\) to be rounded down to 2 and the five largest to be rounded up to 3.  Thus
\[
A_1=A_2=2,\quad A_3=A_4=A_5=A_6=A_7=3,
\]
and the worst error is
\[
\max\{\,2.61-2,\;3-2.65\}=\max\{0.61,\;0.35\}=0.61.
\]
Hence \(M=0.61\) and
\[
100M=61.
\]
\(\boxed{61}\)

\[

\]
and the worst error is
\[
\max\{\,2.61-2,\;3-2.65\}=\max\{0.61,\;0.35\}=0.61.

\[

\(\boxed{61}\)
